attachments.name: Create design system
attachments.content:
We will create a design system in this project.

**Company description:** I'll extract and organize the complete design system from the brief and images for you.

## PixelPlay Design System

### Canvas & Layout
- **Canvas Size**: 1612 × 906px (16:9, fixed - no scrolling)
- **Sidebar Width**: 537px (exactly 1/3 of canvas width)
- **Custom Title Bar**: Slim bar at top with connection status dot (green = connected, grey = disconnected) + minimize/close buttons

---

### Colors

**Primary Palette**
- **Primary/Accent**: `#B3A7FF` (lavender purple)
  - Usage: Active states, selected indicators, primary buttons, Phase Active glow

**Text**
- **Primary Text**: `#1a1a1a` (near-black charcoal)
- **Secondary Text**: `#888888` (muted grey)

**Surfaces**
- **Card Background**: `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)` (30% opacity white)
- **Card Border**: `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4)` (40% opacity white)

**Background**
- **Gradient** (for prototyping): `linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e 0%, #16213e 50%, #0f3460 100%)`
- In production: Full-bleed background image (BG.jpg) with animated soft orbs

---

### Typography

| Role | Size | Weight |
|------|------|--------|
| **Caption / Small Label** | 12px | Regular |
| **Body Text** | 14px | Regular |
| **Body Strong / Emphasized Label** | 14px | Semi-bold |
| **Section Header** | 20px | Semi-bold |
| **Page Title** | 28px | Semi-bold |

---

### Card Style (Glassmorphism)

Every card and panel uses this consistent style:

```css
background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3);
backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4);
border-radius: 16px;
```

- Semi-transparent white background
- Soft backdrop blur effect
- Thin white border
- Background image always shows through

---

### Animations & Motion

**Timing & Easing**

| Animation Type | Duration | Easing | Details |
|----------------|----------|--------|---------|
| **Screen Transitions** | 400ms | ease | Fade between screens |
| **Sidebar Open/Close** | 300ms | ease | Slides in/out from right |
| **Card Entrance** | Staggered | spring bounce | Slide up from 40px below, fade in, 200ms stagger |
| **Pill Selector** | 500ms | spring | White pill slides between options |
| **Sidebar Items** | Staggered | - | Fade and slide up from 20px below, 150ms stagger |

**Animation Patterns**
- **Cards entering screen**: Each card slides up from 40px below and fades in, staggered 200ms apart, with spring bounce feel
- **Sidebar content**: Items reveal bottom-to-top, 150ms stagger
- **Pill selector**: Smooth slide with spring transition
- **Background orbs**: Animate into position on app launch

---

### Navigation Bar

**Specs**
- **Size**: 240 × 40px pill-shaped container
- **Position**: Centered at top of screen
- **Style**: Frosted-glass (same glassmorphism as cards)

**Items**
- Three items: Home, Mouse, Joystick (+ Phase for new feature)
- Each has small icon (18×18px SVG)
- Selected item: solid white pill that smoothly slides between items
- Hover behavior: Label text slides in next to icon; disappears when not hovered (except selected)

**Left Side**
- Brand logo (animated on hover)
- "Pixelplay" text

**Right Side**
- Profile icon button
- Notification icon button  
- Settings icon button

---

### Sidebar Panel

**Structure**
- **Width**: 537px (one-third of screen)
- **Animation**: Slides in from right side
- **Fixed Header**: Title + close button (doesn't scroll)
- **Scrollable Body**: Content scrolls independently
- **Overlay**: Semi-transparent dark overlay covers rest of screen

**Behavior**
- Active trigger button appears "above" overlay (pops out)
- Content animates in: bottom-to-top reveal, 150ms stagger

---

### Components

**1. Sliding Pill Selector**
- Used for 2-3 option selection
- White pill slides under selected option
- 500ms spring transition
- Used in: Navigation, Layer 1/2 switching, Joystick modes (Button/Trigger/Sticks)

**2. Configurator Card**
- Glassmorphism card style
- Represents one physical input
- Contains:
  - Input name
  - Button label chip
  - List of mapped actions
- Tapping opens sidebar with settings

**3. Button Marker**
- Small draggable label chip (e.g., "LT", "L1", "RB")
- Floats over device illustration
- Tapping opens sidebar
- Draggable in Setter Mode

**4. Setter Mode Button**
- Position: Top-right of Mouse and Joystick screens
- States:
  - Active: "Exit Setter"
  - Inactive: "Setter Mode"
- When active: All floating elements become draggable

**5. Reset Button**
- Small button on each screen
- Resets positions of floating elements to defaults
- Also draggable in Setter Mode

---

### Icon Assets

**Requirements**
- **Format**: SVG
- **Size**: 18 × 18px
- **Variants**: 
  - Filled version (for selected state)
  - Outline version (for default/hover)
- **Style**: Must match existing Mouse and Joystick icons

---

### States & Feedback

**Connection Status**
- Green dot: Device connected
- Grey dot: Device disconnected
- Position: Custom title bar

**Conflict Warning**
- Inline on affected card (not popup/modal)
- Small warning icon + caption text "Conflict detected"
- Contained within card boundary

**Toast Notification**
- Position: Bottom-center of screen
- Animation: Slides up and fades in
- Duration: ~3 seconds, then fades out and slides down
- Style: Glassmorphism card style
- Non-blocking

---

### Layout Principles

**No Modals**: All flows happen inside sidebar or as nav items
**Sidebar Width**: Fixed at 537px, no horizontal scrolling
**Floating Elements**: Used on Mouse, Joystick, and Phase screens—arranged around implied device center
**Setter Mode**: New floating elements must be draggable when Setter Mode is active

---

This design system ensures visual consistency across all three new features (Phase Mode, Custom Macros, Profile Switching) while extending the existing PixelPlay interface.

**Figma file (mounted as a virtual filesystem):**

Every .jsx carries a "// node: <id>" header; that id (or the directory's VFS path) is what fig_screenshot accepts.

The .jsx is PSEUDOCODE — an illustrative reconstruction from the binary. Layout, colors, type, spacing and instance-override props are accurate enough to recreate from; it may not compile and may not work. It IS the design source of truth: prefer fig_read + fig_grep over screenshots. Use fig_screenshot SPARINGLY — only for a broad overview of an unfamiliar frame or to spot-check a detail the JSX can't express. Screenshots burn a lot of context; one or two for orientation is fine, one per component is not. Don't screenshot a node you haven't fig_read first.

Caveats: per-character text styles, list markers, deep nested instance swaps and variable aliases are not fully resolved; diamond gradients, NOISE effects and GRID auto-layout are approximated. When the JSX and a screenshot disagree on those specifics, trust the JSX values. Copy SVGs/images out with fig_copy_files (do NOT redraw them by hand). Use the precise designs as reference.

---

Design systems are folders on the file system containing typography guidelines, colors, assets, brand style and tone guides, css styles, and React recreations of UIs, decks, etc. they give design agents the ability to create designs against a company's existing products, and create assets using that company's brand. Design systems should contain real visual assets (logos, brand illustrations, etc), low-level visual foundations (e.g. typography specifics; color system, shadow, border, spacing systems) and also high-level visual ELEMENTS (buttons, full screens) within ui kits.

No need to invoke the create_design_system skill; this is it.

To begin, create a todo list with the tasks below, then follow it:

- Explore provided assets and materials to gain a high-level understanding of the company/product context, the different products represented, etc. Read each asset (codebase, figma, file etc) and see what they do. Find some product copy; examine core screens; find any design system definitions.
- Create a README.md with the high-level understanding of the company/product context, the different products represented, etc. Mention the sources you were given: full Figma links, codebase paths, etc. Do not assume the reader has access, but store in case they do.
- Call set_project_title with a short name derived from the brand/product (e.g. "Acme Design System"). This replaces the generic placeholder so the project is findable.
- IF any slide decks attached, use your repl tool to look at them, extract key assets + text, write to disk.
- Explore the codebase and/or figma design contexts and create a colors_and_type.css file containing CSS vars for both base type + color styles (e.g. fg1, fg2, serif-display, etc) and semantic CSS vars (e.g. h1, h2, code, p). Copy any webfonts or ttfs into fonts/.
- Explore, then update README.md with a CONTENT FUNDAMENTALS section: how is copy written? What is tone, casing, etc? I vs you, etc? are emoji used? What is the vibe? Include specific examples
- Explore, update README.md with VISUAL FOUNDATIONS section that talks about the visual motifs and foundations of the brand. Colors, type, spacing, backgrounds (images? full-bleed? hand-drawn illustrations? repeating patterns/textures? gradients?), animation (easing? fades? bounces? no anims?), hover states (opacity, darker colors, lighter colors?), press states (color? shrink?), borders, inner/outer shadow systems, protection gradients vs capsules, layout rules (fixed elements), use of transparency and blur (when?), color vibe of imagery (warm? cool? b&w? grain?), corner radii, what do cards look like (shadow, rounding, border), etc. whatever else you can think of. answer ALL these questions.
- If you are missing font files, find the nearest match on Google Fonts. Flag this substitution to the user and ask for updated font files.
- As you work, create HTML card files in preview/ that populate the Design System tab. Target ~700×150px each (400px max) — err toward MORE small cards, not fewer dense ones. Split at the sub-concept level: separate cards for primary vs neutral vs semantic colors; display vs body vs mono type; spacing tokens vs a spacing-in-use example; one card per component state cluster. A typical system is 12–20+ cards. Skip titles and framing — the asset name renders OUTSIDE the card, so just show the swatches/specimens/tokens directly with minimal decoration. After writing each batch, call register_assets with items carrying viewport {width: 700, height: <your estimate>}, a one-line subtitle, and a `group` tag so the Design System tab can split cards into sections. Use these groups: "Type" for typography specimens and scales, "Colors" for palettes / color scales / semantic colors, "Spacing" for radii / shadow systems / spacing tokens / elevation, "Components" for buttons / form inputs / cards / badges / menus, "Brand" for logos / imagery / anything that doesn't fit the others. Title-cased, consistent across the batch.
- Copy logos, icons and other visual assets into assets/. update README.md with an ICONOGRAPHY describing the brand's approach to iconography. Answer ALL these and more: are certain icon systems used? is there a builtin icon font? are there SVGs used commonly, or png icons? (if so, copy them in!) Is emoji ever used? Are unicode chars used as icons? Make sure to copy key logos, background images, maybe 1-2 full-bleed generic images, and ALL generic illustrations you find. NEVER draw your own SVGs or generate images; COPY icons programmatically if you can.
- For icons: FIRST copy the codebase's own icon font/sprite/SVGs into assets/ if you can. Otherwise, if the set is CDN-available (e.g. Lucide, Heroicons), link it from CDN. If neither, substitute the closest CDN match (same stroke weight / fill style) and FLAG the substitution. Document usage in ICONOGRAPHY.
- For each product given (E.g. app and website), create UI kits in ui_kits/<product>/{README.md, index.html, Component1.jsx, Component2.jsx}; see the UI kits section. Verify visually. Make one todo list item for each product/surface.
- If you were given a slide template, create sample slides in slides/{index.html, TitleSlide.jsx, ComparisonSlide.jsx, BigQuoteSlide.jsx, etc}. If no sample slides were given, don't create them. Create an HTML file per slide type; if decks were provided, copy their style. Use the visual foundations and bring in logos + other assets. Register each slide HTML via register_assets with viewport {width: 1280, height: 720} so the 16:9 frame scales to fit the card.
- Register each UI kit's index.html as its own card via register_assets with viewport {width: <kit's design width>, height: <above-fold height>} — the declared height caps what's shown, so pick the portion worth previewing.
- Update README.md with a short "index" pointing the reader to the other files available. This should serve as a manifest of the root folder, plus a list of ui kits, etc.
- Create SKILL.md file (details below)
- You are done! The Design System tab shows every registered card. Do NOT summarize your output; just mention CAVEATS (e.g. things you were unable to do or unsure) and have a CLEAR, BOLD ASK for the user to help you ITERATE to make things PERFECT.

UI kit details:
- UI Kits are high-fidelity visual + interaction recreations of interfaces. They cut corners on functionality -- they are not 'real production code' -- but they provide high-fidelity UI components. Your UI kits should be pixel-perfect recreations, created by reading the original UI code if possible, or using figma's get-design-context. They should be modular and reusable, so they can easily be pieced together for real designs. UI kits should recreate key screens in the product as click-thru prototypes. a UI kit's index.html must look like a typical view of the product. These are recreations, not storybooks.
- To start, update the todo list to contain these steps for each product: (1) Explore codebase + components in Figma (design context) and code, (2) Create 3-5 core screens for each product (e.g. homepage or app) with interactive click-thru components, (3) Iterate visually on the designs 1-2x, cross-referencing with design context.
- Figure out the core products from this company/codebase. There may be one, or a few. (e.g. mobile app, marketing website, docs website).
- Each UI kit must contain JSX components (well-factored; small, neat) for core UI elements (e.g. sidebars, composers, file panels, hero units, headers, footers, buttons, fields, menus, blog posts, video players, settings screens, login, etc).
- The index.html file should demonstrate an interactive version of the UI (e.g a chat app would show you a login screen, let you create a chat, send a message, etc, as fake)
- You should get the visuals exactly right, using design context or codebase import. Don't copy component implementations exactly; make simple mainly-cosmetic versions. It's important to copy.
- Focus on good component coverage, not replicating every single section in a design.
- Do not invent new designs for UI kits. The job of the UI kit is to replicate the existing design, not create a new one. Copy the design, don't reinvent it. If you do not see it in the project, omit, or leave purposely blank with a disclaimer.

Guidance
- Run independently without stopping unless there's a crucial blocker (E.g. lack of Figma access to a pasted link; lack of codebase access).
- When creating slides and UI kits, avoid cutting corners on iconography; instead, copy icon assets in! Do not create halfway representations of iconography using hand-rolled SVG, emoji, etc.
- CRITICAL: Do not recreate UIs from screenshots alone unless you have no other choice! Use the codebase, or Figma's get-design-context, as a source of truth. Screenshots are much lossier than code; use screenshots as a high-level guide but always find components in the codebase if you can!
- Avoid these visual motifs unless you are sure you see them in the codebase or Figma: bluish-purple gradients, emoji cards, cards with rounded corners and colored left-border only
- Avoid reading SVGs -- this is a waste of context! If you know their usage, just copy them and then reference them.
- When using Figma, use get-design-context to understand the design system and components being used. Screenshots are ONLY useful for high-level guidance. Make sure to expand variables and child components to get their content, too. (get_variable_defs)
- Create these files in the ROOT of the project unless asked not to. For example, README.md should be at the root, not in a folder!
- Stop if key resources are unnecessible: ff a codebase was attached or mentioned, but you are unable to access it via local_ls, etc, you MUST stop and ask the user to re-attach it using the Import menu. These get reattached often; do not complete a design system if you get a disconnect! Similarly, if a Figma url is inaccessible, stop and ask the user to rectify. NEVER go ahead spending tons of time making a design system if you cannot access all the resources the user gave you.

SKILL.md
- When you are done, we should make this file cross-compatible with Agent SKills in case the user wants to download it and use it in Claude Code.
- Create a SKILL.md file like this:

<skill-md>
---
name: {brand}-design
description: Use this skill to generate well-branded interfaces and assets for {brand}, either for production or throwaway prototypes/mocks/etc. Contains essential design guidelines, colors, type, fonts, assets, and UI kit components for protoyping.
user-invocable: true
---

Read the README.md file within this skill, and explore the other available files.
If creating visual artifacts (slides, mocks, throwaway prototypes, etc), copy assets out and create static HTML files for the user to view. If working on production code, you can copy assets and read the rules here to become an expert in designing with this brand.
If the user invokes this skill without any other guidance, ask them what they want to build or design, ask some questions, and act as an expert designer who outputs HTML artifacts _or_ production code, depending on the need.
</skill-md>

**10 design system cards** in the Design System tab covering colors, type, components, motion tokens, and spacing — plus a full interactive UI kit.

**Caveats:**
- 🔤 **Font substitution:** Production uses `Segoe UI Variable` (Windows built-in). The kit uses **Nunito** from Google Fonts as the closest available substitute. If you have the actual font files, drop them into `fonts/` and I'll update the CSS.
- 🖼 **Background image:** The `bg.png` copied from Figma is a cropped/partial view. If you have the full `BG.jpg` used in production, share it and I'll swap it in.
- 🕹 **Joystick screen:** Left as a placeholder — no Figma designs were available for it in the focused frames.
- 📐 **Canvas size discrepancy:** The brief says 1612×906px but all Figma screens are 1280×720px. The UI kit uses 1280×720.

**To make it perfect:**
> **Can you share the production `BG.jpg` background, the actual `Segoe UI Variable` font files, and any joystick screen designs?** That'll close the remaining gaps.

input.content:
# PixelPlay Design System

## About

**PixelPlay** (by PixelPaws Labs) is a Windows desktop application for configuring gaming peripherals — primarily a gaming mouse and game controller. The app lets users map buttons, configure DPI/movement, set up layers, manage Phase Mode (a multi-mode overlay system), create Custom Macros, and switch between device profiles.

The UI is a fixed 1280×720px window (recommended app size) with a custom Windows-style title bar. It is built for Windows desktop using Fluent Design conventions (Segoe UI Variable, Mica-like backgrounds, acrylic/glass surfaces).

---

## Sources

- **Figma:** `PIXELPLAY REDESIGN REV1.fig` (mounted as VFS; 3 pages: Page-1, Playground, Screens; 168 top-level frames)
- **Design brief:** Provided via structured system prompt (canvas, colors, animation specs, component list)

No external codebase was attached.

---

## Content Fundamentals

### Voice & Tone
- **Concise, technical, friendly.** Copy is short and action-oriented. Labels are noun phrases or verb phrases — never full sentences.
- **No emoji.** Icons are used instead.
- **Sentence case** for all labels and UI text (e.g. "Reset to default", "Power saver mode", "Left Button").
- **Title case** only for product/mode names (e.g. "Phase Mode", "Setter Mode", "Layer 1").
- **No filler text.** Every word earns its place. Secondary text is grey and minimal.
- **"You" / second-person** implied: actions are always described from the user's perspective.
- Mapping actions are described as verbs: "Click", "Back", "Scroll up", "Scroll down".
- Status messages are plain: "Up to date", "35 hours remaining", "Optimized performance".

### Example copy patterns
- Card labels: **"Left Button"**, **"Right Button"**, **"Left Touchpad"**
- Action labels: **"Click"**, **"Back"**, **"Swipe up"**, **"Scroll up"**
- Status: **"50%"**, **"35 hours remaining"**, **"26.25.3"**, **"current version"**
- Bottom bar tip: *"You can now choose smart actions from the mapping. Learn more"*
- Button labels: **"Setter Mode"**, **"Exit Setter"**, **"Reset to default"**

---

## Visual Foundations

### Colors

| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `--accent` | `#B3A7FF` / `rgb(179,167,255)` | Active pill, selected states, primary accent |
| `--accent-strong` | `rgb(147,129,255)` | Action labels (mapped button text) |
| `--accent-dim` | `rgba(147,129,255,0.85)` | Subtle accent fills |
| `--accent-muted` | `rgba(179,167,255,0.3)` | Toggle track (on state) |
| `--fg-1` | `rgb(34,34,34)` | Primary text |
| `--fg-2` | `rgb(161,161,161)` | Secondary/caption text |
| `--fg-3` | `rgb(131,118,211)` | Action value labels (purple-grey) |
| `--surface-card` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.5)` | Card background |
| `--surface-sidebar` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.2)` | Sidebar/panel background |
| `--surface-nav` | `rgb(239,237,248)` | Nav pill background (lavender tint) |
| `--border-card` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` | Card/nav border |
| `--border-subtle` | `rgba(201,192,255,0.5)` | Subtle purple border |
| `--danger` | `rgb(255,80,80)` | Error/conflict states |
| `--bg-fallback` | `linear-gradient(135deg,#1a1a2e,#16213e,#0f3460)` | Dark gradient fallback |

### Typography

- **Primary font:** `Segoe UI Variable` (semibold for headers/labels, regular for body/captions)
- **Secondary font:** `Inter` (used for nav pill labels, Phase Mode branding)
- Google Fonts substitution: `Segoe UI Variable` → **`Nunito`** (closest available match for weight + roundness)

| Role | Font | Size | Weight |
|---|---|---|---|
| Page display | Inter | 86px | Regular |
| Page title | Segoe UI Variable | 28px | Semibold |
| Section header | Segoe UI Variable | 20px | Semibold |
| Card label | Segoe UI Variable | 14px | Semibold |
| Body / label | Segoe UI Variable | 14px | Regular |
| Caption | Segoe UI Variable | 12px | Regular |
| Micro label | Segoe UI Variable | 10px | Regular / Semibold |
| Nav pill text | Inter | 12px | Regular |

### Backgrounds

- **Production:** Full-bleed background image (`assets/images/bg.png`) — a soft, bokeh-lit dark purple/navy scene. Animated soft color orbs layer on top on launch.
- **Surface:** White-tinted `rgb(255,255,255)` base, then semi-transparent cards float above.
- **In Figma mockups:** Clean white background used for legibility.
- Background always shows through card surfaces (glassmorphism).

### Cards (Glassmorphism)

```css
background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.5);
backdrop-filter: blur(4px);
border-radius: 20px;
```

- Sidebar cards use a stronger blur: `blur(20px) saturate(120%)`, border `1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)`, radius `16px`
- No drop shadow on standard cards; inner white glow on selected nav pill: `inset 0 0 7px rgba(255,255,255,0.4)`

### Motion & Animation

| Animation | Duration | Easing |
|---|---|---|
| Screen transitions | 400ms | ease |
| Sidebar open/close | 300ms | ease |
| Card entrance (stagger) | ~200ms stagger | spring bounce |
| Pill selector slide | 500ms | spring |
| Sidebar items reveal | ~150ms stagger | ease-out |
| Background orbs | On launch | ease |

- Cards enter by sliding up 40px and fading in, staggered 200ms apart
- Pill selector slides smoothly under selected nav item
- Sidebar content reveals bottom-to-top with 150ms stagger per item

### Hover & Press States

- Hover: slight opacity increase or color shift (subtle)
- Nav items: label text slides in on hover, disappears when not hovered (except selected)
- Selected nav pill: solid white pill with white inner glow
- Active Phase pill: solid lavender `#B3A7FF` with white text and glow

### Corner Radii

| Element | Radius |
|---|---|
| Cards | 20px |
| Sidebar panels | 16px |
| Nav pill container | 20px (pill) |
| Button tags (LB/RB) | 10px (pill) |
| Small toggle | ~18px (pill) |
| Accent buttons | 20px (pill) |

### Borders & Shadows

- Card border: none (glass surface self-defines)
- Nav pill: `1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)`
- Selected pill: `1px solid rgb(255,255,255)` + inner glow
- No heavy drop shadows; depth is created via translucency layers

### Iconography

See ICONOGRAPHY section below.

### Layout Rules

- **Canvas:** 1280×720px fixed, no scroll
- **Title bar:** 32px slim custom bar at top
- **Nav bar:** Full-width, 40px height, centered nav pill (320–340px wide)
- **Sidebar:** 433px from right edge, full height minus title bar
- **Floating button markers:** Positioned around the device illustration (absolute), draggable in Setter Mode
- **No modals** — all flows use sidebar or new nav screens

---

## Iconography

PixelPlay uses **custom SVG icons** (inline or as SVG files) at 18×18px for navigation items:

- `assets/icons/logo-union.svg` — PixelPaws cat logo (paw/face mark), used in nav bar branding
- `assets/icons/nav-mouse.svg` — Mouse icon for nav pill

Navigation icons use filled SVG paths in `rgb(34,34,34)` for default state and `rgb(255,255,255)` for selected state.

**Button tag chips** (LB, RB, LT, RT, etc.) are text labels inside small pill-shaped containers — not icon assets.

**Phosphor Icons** (via CDN) are used for system icons like battery (`BatteryVerticalLow`), leaf (`Leaf`), clock (`ClockClockwise`, `ClockCounterClockwise`), carets. These render as SVG fills in `rgba(147,129,255,0.5)`.

No emoji. No unicode char icons. No icon fonts.

### Icon sizes
- Nav icons: 18×18px
- Card system icons: 24–28px, lavender fill at 50% opacity
- Caret/utility icons: 12px

---

## Files

```
README.md                  — This file
SKILL.md                   — Agent skill definition
colors_and_type.css        — CSS custom properties for all tokens
assets/
  icons/
    logo-union.svg         — PixelPaws cat logo
    nav-mouse.svg          — Mouse nav icon
  images/
    bg.png                 — Full-bleed background (dark purple bokeh)
    mouse-hero.png         — Mouse product hero image (front/iso view)
    paw-logo.png           — Paw logo variant 1
    paw-logo-2.png         — Paw logo variant 2
preview/
  colors-primary.html      — Primary + accent color palette
  colors-semantic.html     — Semantic / text / surface colors
  type-scale.html          — Typography scale specimen
  type-fonts.html          — Font pairings specimen
  cards.html               — Card styles (glass, sidebar, nav pill)
  buttons.html             — Button tag chips + pill buttons
  nav-bar.html             — Nav bar component
  sidebar.html             — Sidebar panel component
  motion.html              — Animation tokens reference
  spacing.html             — Corner radii + spacing tokens
ui_kits/
  app/
    README.md              — App UI kit notes
    index.html             — Interactive PixelPlay app prototype
    NavBar.jsx             — Nav bar component
    Card.jsx               — Glassmorphism card
    Sidebar.jsx            — Sliding sidebar panel
    ButtonTag.jsx          — Button marker chip
    HomeScreen.jsx         — Home screen
    MouseScreen.jsx        — Mouse configurator screen
```

# PixelPlay App UI Kit

Interactive click-through prototype of the PixelPlay Windows desktop app.

## Screens

- **Home** — Device overview with battery, power saver, and software update cards
- **Mouse** — Button configurator with floating card labels over device illustration
- **Joystick** — Placeholder (connect controller prompt)

## Components

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `NavBar.jsx` | Top nav bar with logo, pill nav, and side icon buttons |
| `Card.jsx` | `GlassCard`, `ButtonTag`, `ConfigCard`, `StatCard` |
| `Sidebar.jsx` | Sliding right panel with staggered row reveal and toggle |
| `HomeScreen.jsx` | Home screen with hero image and stat cards |
| `MouseScreen.jsx` | Mouse mapping screen with floating config cards |

## Usage

Open `index.html` in a browser. The app scales to fit any viewport.
Click any configurator card to open the sidebar panel.
Use the nav bar to switch screens.

## Notes

- Background image (`assets/images/bg.png`) is shown at low opacity behind the white surface
- The production app uses `Segoe UI Variable` (Windows built-in); this kit substitutes `Nunito` from Google Fonts
- `Inter` is used exactly as designed for nav labels and Phase branding

# Macro Gallery Interactive Prototype - Build Documentation **Target**: PixelPlay Macro Manager sidebar panel with pre-defined macro gallery --- ## Overview Build an interactive HTML prototype (1612×906px) showing the Macro Manager sidebar with a new "Macro Gallery" feature. Users can browse pre-defined macros by category and import them with one click. --- ## User Flow ``` Macro Manager (List State) └── [Browse Gallery] button ↓ Macro Gallery View (replaces sidebar content) ├── Category pills (All, Gaming, Productivity, Creative, Streaming, Dev) ├── Horizontal scrolling carousel of macro cards ├── Selected card detail panel below carousel └── [+ Import] button ↓ Back to Macro Manager (List State) with new macro added └── Success toast: "Macro imported" ``` --- ## Layout Specs ### Sidebar Dimensions - Width: **537px** (one-third of 1612px canvas) - Slides in from **right** - Fixed header (no scroll) - Body content scrolls independently ### Macro Gallery Structure ``` ┌─ Macro Gallery ──────────────────────────┐ │ [← Back] [× Close] │ ← Fixed header (60px) │─────────────────────────────────────────│ │ │ │ 🏷️ Filters (horizontal pill selector) │ ← 44px tall │ [All] Gaming Productivity Creative ... │ │ │ │ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ │ │ │ Macro │ │ Macro │ │ Macro │ → │ ← Carousel (180px tall cards) │ │ Card 1 │ │ Card 2 │ │ Card 3 │ │ Horizontal scroll │ └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ │ │ │ │ ┌─ Selected Macro Detail ──────────────┐│ │ │ Quick Melee [+ Import] ││ ← Detail panel │ │ Switch weapon → Melee → Switch back ││ (auto-height, min 200px) │ │ 3 steps · Gaming ││ │ │ ││ │ │ Perfect for fast-paced shooters when ││ │ │ you need instant melee without... ││ │ └───────────────────────────────────────┘│ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` --- ## Component Specs ### 1. Category Pills (Filter Bar) - Container: `rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`, border-radius 100px, height 44px - Pills: 6 categories - All (default active) - Gaming 🎮 - Productivity 💼 - Creative 🎨 - Streaming 🎬 - Dev 🔧 - Active pill: white background, `#1a1a1a` text (bold) - Inactive pill: transparent, `#9b8fcf` text - White pill slides with spring animation: `cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)`, 500ms ### 2. Carousel Cards - Card size: **240px wide × 180px tall** - Glassmorphism style: ```css background: rgba(255,255,255,0.3); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); ``` - Spacing: 16px gap between cards - Hover state: subtle lift (`translateY(-4px)`) + stronger shadow - Active/selected state: border changes to `#B3A7FF` (2px solid) - Content layout: ``` ┌────────────────────┐ │ 🎮 Gaming │ ← category badge (caption, 12px) │ │ │ Quick Melee │ ← name (body-strong, 14px bold) │ 3 steps │ ← step count (caption, grey) │ │ │ [Visual preview] │ ← step icons or mini diagram └────────────────────┘ ``` ### 3. Success Toast - Bottom-center of canvas - Slides up from `translateY(100px)` + fade in (300ms ease) - Glassmorphism card, padding 16px 24px - Text: "Macro imported" (body text, #1a1a1a) - Auto-dismiss after 3s (slide down + fade out) --- ## Pre-defined Macro Data Use this dataset (15 macros across 5 categories): ```javascript const macroGallery = [ // Gaming { id: 1, name: "Quick Melee", category: "Gaming", steps: 3, breakdown: "Switch weapon → Melee → Switch back", description: "Perfect for fast-paced shooters when you need instant melee without fumbling through weapon wheel." }, { id: 2, name: "Tactical Crouch", category: "Gaming", steps: 3, breakdown: "Crouch → Wait 100ms → Stand", description: "Quick crouch-slide for dodging shots while maintaining momentum." }, { id: 3, name: "Reload Cancel", category: "Gaming", steps: 3, breakdown: "Reload → Switch weapon → Switch back", description: "Skip reload animation by weapon swapping. Transition back to Macro Manager List State (same fade transition) 3. New macro appears at top of list with subtle highlight flash 4. Success toast slides up from bottom-center 5. Toast auto-dismisses after 3s ### 6. Back Arrow Click - **Trigger**: User clicks ← back arrow in header - **Action**: Return to Macro Manager List State (no import) --- ## Animation Timings ```css --anim-screen-fade: 400ms ease; --anim-sidebar: 300ms ease; --anim-pill: 500ms cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1); /* spring */ --anim-card-select: 200ms ease; --anim-toast-in: 300ms ease; --anim-toast-out: 300ms ease; ``` ### Card Carousel Entry Animation When gallery first opens: - Cards stagger in from bottom (translateY(40px) → 0) - Fade in (opacity 0 → 1) - Stagger delay: 80ms between each card - Spring bounce feel: `cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)` --- ## Design System Tokens (Use Throughout) ```css /* Colors */ --color-primary: #B3A7FF; --color-text-primary: #1a1a1a; --color-text-secondary: #888888; --color-layer-inactive: #9b8fcf; --color-card-bg: rgba(255,255,255,0.3); --color-card-border: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); /* Typography */ --text-caption: 12px, weight 400; --text-body: 14px, weight 400; --text-body-strong: 14px, weight 600; --text-section: 20px, weight 600; --text-title: 28px, weight 600; /* Font */ font-family: 'Inter', 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; ``` --- ## HTML Structure Template ```html PixelPlay - Macro Gallery Prototype
Macro imported
``` --- ## Key Implementation Notes 1. **Carousel scroll behavior**: Use CSS `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` on carousel container for smooth card snapping 2. **Category filtering**: Filter macro array by category, then re-render carousel. Use CSS transitions for smooth card entry/exit 3. **Import logic**: ```javascript function importMacro(macroId) { const macro = macroGallery.find(m => m.id === macroId); userMacros.unshift(macro); // Add to top of user list closeGallery(); showToast('Macro imported'); } ``` 4. **State persistence**: Store `userMacros` array to show imported macros in List State 5. **Responsive carousel**: Calculate visible cards based on 537px width minus padding --- ## Testing Checklist - [ ] Sidebar slides in from right (300ms) - [ ] Category pills filter carousel correctly - [ ] White pill slides with spring animation - [ ] Cards scroll horizontally with momentum - [ ] Selected card shows purple border - [ ] Detail panel updates on card click - [ ] Import button adds macro to list - [ ] Success toast appears bottom-center - [ ] Toast auto-dismisses after 3s - [ ] Back arrow returns to list state - [ ] Card carousel stagger animation on open --- **Output**: Single self-contained HTML file, ready to open in browser at 1612×906px viewport.

toolCall.output:
--- Script output ---
PixelPlay - Design Brief v1
For: Freelance UI/UX Designer
Project: Pixelplay Desktop Application
Prepared by: Phase Mouse
Date: 2026-04-06
A Note Before You Start
This brief asks for two things: visual designs and interactive prototypes. Instead of building
interactions inside Figma, you'll use an AI tool called Claude to bring your designs to life as
clickable, animated prototypes. There's a full guide on how to do this at the end of this
document - you don't need any coding experience.
1. Project Summary
PixelPlay is a desktop companion app for a hybrid mouse/gamepad device. The app is already
fully built and running. This brief covers three new features that need to be designed. You are
extending the app - not redesigning it.
Your job: understand the existing visual style, design the new screens and components, then
use Claude to produce working interactive prototypes.
2. Existing Design Language
All new work must look and feel like it belongs to what's already there. Study this section
carefully before designing anything.
Canvas & Window
- Size: 1612 × 906px (16:9, fixed - no scrolling on main screens)
- No OS title bar. The app has its own slim custom title bar at the top showing a
connection status dot (green = connected, grey = disconnected) and minimize/close
buttons.

-- 1 of 12 --

Visual Style - Glassmorphism
Every card and panel in the app uses the same frosted-glass look:
- Semi-transparent white background (about 30% opacity)
- Soft backdrop blur effect behind the card
- Thin white border (about 40% opacity)
- The background image (BG.jpg) always shows through
Background & Atmosphere
- Full-bleed background image with two large soft orbs (blurred color shapes) that animate
into position when you open the app
- No blur filter on the background image itself - it's crisp
- The orbs are purely decorative and add depth
Navigation Bar
- Centered at the top of the screen
- A 240×40px pill-shaped container with a frosted-glass style
- Three items inside: Home, Mouse, Joystick - each with a small icon
- The selected item has a solid white pill that smoothly slides between items when you
switch
- On hover, the item's label text slides in next to the icon; it disappears when not hovered
(except for the selected item)
- Left side of the nav bar: brand logo (animated on hover) + "Pixelplay" text
- Right side: Profile, Notification, and Settings icon buttons
Sidebar Panel
- Slides in from the right side of the screen
- Takes up exactly one-third of the screen width (~537px)
- Has a fixed header (title + close button) that doesn't scroll
- Content below the header scrolls independently
- A semi-transparent dark overlay covers the rest of the screen behind it
- When a sidebar trigger button is active (sidebar is open for that button), the button
appears visually "above" the overlay - it pops out
Animations & Motion (describe these to Claude later)
- Screen transitions: 0.4 second fade between screens
- Cards entering a screen: Stagger in from bottom to top, 0.2 seconds apart, slight
spring bounce
- Sidebar content: Items reveal from bottom to top, 0.15 seconds apart

-- 2 of 12 --

- Pill selectors: The white pill slides smoothly between options with a spring feel
- Sidebar open/close: Width animates from 0 to one-third in 0.3 seconds
Typography (sizes to use)
Role 	Size 	Weight
Small label / caption 	12px 	Regular
Body text 	14px 	Regular
Emphasized label 	14px 	Semi-bold
Section header 	20px 	Semi-bold
Page title 	28px 	Semi-bold
Colors
- Primary / Accent: #B3A7FF (soft lavender purple) - used for active states, selected
indicators, primary buttons
- Text: Near-black charcoal for primary, muted grey for secondary/helper text
- Cards: White at ~30% opacity
- Borders: White at ~40% opacity
Recurring Components
Sliding Pill Selector - used everywhere a user picks between 2–3 options. A pill slides under
the selected option. Used in the nav, in Layer 1/2 switching, and in Joystick mode switching
(Button / Trigger / Sticks).
Configurator Card - a glassmorphism card that represents one physical input on the device
(e.g., "Left Touchpad"). Shows the input name, a button label chip, and a list of mapped actions.
Tapping the card opens the sidebar with settings for that input.
Button Marker - a small draggable label chip (e.g., "LT", "L1") that floats over the device
illustration. Tapping it opens the sidebar. In Setter Mode, these can be dragged to reposition.
Setter Mode - a toggle button (top-right of Mouse and Joystick screens) that, when active, lets
the user drag all floating elements to reposition them. When active the button reads "Exit
Setter"; when inactive it reads "Setter Mode".
Reset Button - a small button on each screen that resets the positions of all floating elements
back to their defaults. Also draggable in Setter Mode.

-- 3 of 12 --

Current Screens
Home Screen - Device status cards arranged in a row: battery level, power mode, software
update status.
Mouse Screen - Floating cards over an implied device: Left Button card, Left Touchpad card,
DPI/Movement card, an "LT" button marker, and a Layer 1/2 pill selector at the bottom.
Joystick Screen - Floating elements with a Joystick mode pill selector (Button / Trigger /
Sticks). Content changes based on mode:
- Button mode: L1, L2, R1, R2 button markers
- Trigger mode: LT and RT trigger bars (show current trigger value)
- Sticks mode: Left and right stick configuration circles with deadzone ring
3. Features to Design
Feature 1 - Phase Mode Screen
What it is
Phase Mode is a new operating mode where the device works as both a mouse and a
gamepad at the same time. The user needs a dedicated screen to see and configure which
mouse action and which gamepad action are assigned to each physical input simultaneously.
Example: the left touchpad scrolls (mouse role) and also acts as a left joystick (gamepad role) at
the same time.
Navigation
Add Phase Mode as a 4th item in the existing nav pill. The pill will need to expand slightly to fit
four items comfortably.
- Icon needed: A new icon representing the hybrid state - something that visually merges
mouse and gamepad (e.g., two overlapping shapes, a split-device silhouette, or a
"merge" symbol). Deliver both a filled version (for when it's selected) and an outline
version (for default/hover), as SVG files, 18×18px - matching the style of the existing
Mouse and Joystick icons.
- Label: "Phase"

-- 4 of 12 --

Screen Layout
Use the same floating-element composition as the Mouse and Joystick screens - elements
floating around an implied center (device illustration area).
Elements to design:
1. Setter Mode button - top-right, identical to Mouse and Joystick screens.
2. Dual-Role Input Cards - the main content of this screen. Each card represents one
physical input that has a dual role. The card is split into two columns:
- Left column: Mouse Role - shows what the mouse action is (e.g., "Scroll Up")
- Right column: Gamepad Role - shows what the gamepad action is (e.g., "Left
Stick")
- The card header shows the input name (e.g., "Left Touchpad") and a button label
chip
- Tapping the left column opens the sidebar with mouse-role settings
- Tapping the right column opens the sidebar with gamepad-role settings
- Use the same glassmorphism card style as existing Configurator Cards
3. Phase Active Indicator - a clear badge or pill that communicates Phase Mode is
currently running on the device. Suggested: a small pill with a soft glow in #B3A7FF that
reads "Phase Active". This is a status indicator, not a button.
4. Conflict Warning State - design a visual state for when two mapped actions are
incompatible. This should appear inline on the affected card (not a popup or modal). Use
a small warning icon + short caption text like "Conflict detected". Keep it contained to the
card.
5. Layer 1 / Layer 2 pill selector - same sliding pill component used on the Mouse screen.
Position at the bottom center of the screen.
6. Reset Button - same as other screens, draggable in Setter Mode.
Sidebar Panels for Phase Mode
Design two new sidebar panels (same one-third width, fixed header + scrollable body):
- "Mouse Role Settings" - settings for the mouse-side of the selected input (a focused
subset of the existing Mouse Settings panel - sensitivity, scroll behavior, gesture
mapping)

-- 5 of 12 --

- "Gamepad Role Settings" - settings for the gamepad-side of the selected input (a
focused subset of existing Joystick Settings - deadzone, response curve, button
assignment)
Both should follow the existing sidebar animation style: items reveal bottom-to-top, 0.15s
stagger.
Feature 2 - Custom Macro Support
What it is
Users can create named macros - sequences of actions (key presses, mouse clicks, delays,
text) - and assign those macros to any button on the device. A macro lets the user program a
single button press to do multiple things in a specific order.
Where it lives - Two places:
A. Macro Manager (new sidebar panel)
Accessible from a button on the Home screen (add it alongside the existing "Home Settings"
and "Device Info" buttons), or from the Settings icon in the nav bar top-right.
Design the following states for this sidebar panel:
Empty State
- A simple illustration or icon in the center of the content area
- Body text: "No macros yet"
- A full-width primary-colored button: "+ Create Macro"
List State (macros exist)
- Scrollable list of macro cards, each showing:
- Macro name (emphasized label size)
- Step count in small grey text below (e.g., "4 steps")
- Edit icon button on the right
- Delete icon button on the right (with a destructive/red tint)
- A "+ Create Macro" button pinned to the bottom of the sidebar (always visible, not
scrolled away)
Macro Editor State This opens in place of the list (within the same sidebar - no new panel):

-- 6 of 12 --

- Sidebar header changes to "New Macro" or the macro's name
- Inline editable name field at the top
- Step list: each step is a row with:
- A type label chip (e.g., "Key Press", "Delay", "Mouse Click", "Type Text")
- The step value (e.g., "Ctrl+C", "200ms", "Left Click", "Hello world")
- A drag handle on the left for reordering
- A delete button on the right
- "+ Add Step" button below the list - tapping it shows an inline picker within the sidebar
(not a new panel):
- Four options: Key Press / Mouse Click / Delay / Type Text
- Selecting one adds a new step row of that type
- "Save" and "Cancel" buttons at the bottom of the sidebar
B. Macro Assignment in Button Mapping
Wherever a user can choose what a button does (in any settings sidebar panel), add "Macro" as
a selectable action type in the action list. When selected, a secondary list appears showing all
saved macros by name. The user picks one.
Design this as an addition to the action-picker pattern. The macro option should be visually
grouped separately from native actions or have a small "Macro" label/chip to distinguish it.
Feature 3 - Software-Based Profile Switching
What it is
Users can save named Profiles (complete sets of button mappings) and set rules for which
profile activates automatically when a specific application becomes the active window on their
computer. For example: when Photoshop is open and in focus, the device automatically
switches to the "Design" profile.
Where it lives - Three parts:
A. Profile Manager (new sidebar panel)
Accessible from the Settings icon in the nav bar top-right, or a dedicated button.
Profile List State:
- The currently active profile is visually highlighted (primary color dot or border, "Active"
badge)
- List of all profiles, each showing:

-- 7 of 12 --

- Profile name (emphasized label)
- App rule count in small grey text (e.g., "2 apps" or "No rules set")
- Edit button
- Delete button (hidden or disabled when only one profile exists)
- "+ New Profile" button at the bottom
Profile Editor State (opens in place of the list):
- Editable name field at the top
- A read-only summary of what's configured in this profile (e.g., "6 buttons mapped, 2
macros assigned" - shown as small chips or a caption)
- App Rules section - a sub-list within the editor:
- Each rule row shows: app icon (generic placeholder if unknown) + app name +
process name in small grey text
- Remove button per row
- "+ Add App Rule" button below the list
B. Add App Rule Flow (inline, within sidebar)
When the user taps "+ Add App Rule", the sidebar content area transitions to a rule-adding view
(no new panel):
- A search/filter field at the top: "Search apps..."
- A list of currently running applications below it (populated from the OS), each
showing: app icon + app name
- Tapping one selects it and returns to the profile editor with the new rule added
- A "Enter manually" option at the bottom for apps that aren't currently running - opens a
simple text field for the process name
- "Cancel" link to go back without adding a rule
C. Active Profile Indicator
The current profile name should always be visible somewhere in the persistent UI. Design two
layout options and let the developer choose:
- Option A: A small labeled pill in the navbar right side (between the nav pill and the icon
buttons) - shows the profile name with a thin border. Subtle, always visible.
- Option B: The profile name appears in the custom title bar alongside the connection
status dot.
When the profile auto-switches due to an app rule, the indicator should briefly animate to signal
the change (a quick pulse or color flash in #B3A7FF - describe this to Claude when building the
prototype).

-- 8 of 12 --

D. Auto-Switch Toast Notification
When the profile switches automatically, show a small non-blocking notification toast:
- Appears from the bottom-center of the screen, slides up and fades in
- Shows: "Switched to [Profile Name]" - body text size
- Stays for about 3 seconds then fades out and slides back down
- Uses glassmorphism card style (same as all other cards)
- Should not interrupt anything the user is doing
4. Deliverables
For each of the three features:
1. High-fidelity screens at 1612 × 906px canvas
- All states documented (empty, populated, warning/conflict, loading where it
makes sense)
- Sidebar panels shown at 537px wide (one-third of 1612px)
2. Annotations - notes on spacing, font sizes, and colors. Use the token names from
Section 2 where possible. Flag anything new.
3. New icon assets - SVG files for the Phase Mode nav icon (filled + outline variants,
18×18px)
4. Interactive prototype - built with Claude (see Section 5 below)
5. Building Interactive Prototypes with Claude
Instead of using Figma's built-in prototype/animation tools, you'll use Claude - an AI assistant -
to turn your designs into clickable, animated web prototypes. Here's everything you need to
know.
What is Claude?
Claude is an AI you talk to in plain English. You describe what you want, and it writes the code
for you. You don't need to understand the code - you just need to be able to describe your
design clearly. The prototype it produces will run in any web browser.

-- 9 of 12 --

How to Access Claude
Go to claude.ai and create a free account. Use the chat interface - it's like messaging an
assistant.
The Basic Workflow
1. You design a screen or component in your design tool
2. You open Claude and describe what you designed
3. Claude produces an HTML file with the design rendered and animated
4. You open the file in your browser and test it
5. You tell Claude what to fix or adjust
6. Repeat until it looks right
How to Talk to Claude - Tips
Be specific and visual. Don't say "make it look nice." Say "the card has a white background at
30% opacity, a blur effect behind it, a thin white border, rounded corners, and a subtle drop
shadow."
Describe motion in plain English. For example: "when the user clicks a nav item, a white pill
smoothly slides under the new item over 0.5 seconds with a slight bounce at the end" - Claude
will translate this into working animation.
Give it the design system upfront. Start every new prototype session by pasting the following
block into Claude before describing anything else:
Paste this at the start of every Claude session:
I'm building an interactive prototype for a desktop app called PixelPlay. The canvas
is 1612×906px. Here is the design system to use throughout:
Colors:
- Primary/accent: #B3A7FF (lavender purple)
- Text primary: #1a1a1a
- Text secondary: #888888
- Card background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)
- Card border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4)
Typography:

-- 10 of 12 --

- Caption / small label: 12px regular
- Body: 14px regular
- Body strong / emphasized label: 14px semi-bold
- Section header: 20px semi-bold
- Page title: 28px semi-bold
Card style (used everywhere):
- Background: rgba(255,255,255,0.3)
- Backdrop filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%)
- Border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4)
- Border radius: 16px
Animations:
- Screen fade: 400ms ease
- Sidebar open/close: 300ms ease, slides in from the right
- Cards entering screen: each card slides up from 40px below and fades in,
staggered 200ms apart, with a spring bounce feel
- Pill selector: white pill slides between options with a 500ms spring transition
- Sidebar items: fade and slide up from 20px below, staggered 150ms apart
Background: a full-bleed image. For prototyping, use a dark gradient:
linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e 0%, #16213e 50%, #0f3460
100%)
Iterate freely. If Claude gets something wrong, describe exactly what's different from what you
expected. For example: "the sidebar is opening from the left but it should open from the right" or
"the card is too opaque, make the background more see-through."
One component at a time. Don't try to describe the entire app at once. Build screen by screen,
component by component.
Example Prompt Structure
Here's a template for describing a new screen to Claude after pasting the design system:
"Now I want to build the [screen name] screen. Here's what it contains:

-- 11 of 12 --

[List each element, where it sits on the screen, what it looks like, and what happens
when you interact with it]
When the user clicks [X], [Y] should happen with [describe the animation].
Please build this as a single HTML file I can open in my browser."
Sharing Prototypes
Once Claude produces an HTML file, save it and you can:
- Open it directly in Chrome or Firefox
- Send the file to the client/developer for review
- Ask Claude to adjust anything that doesn't look or feel right
6. Design Constraints
- No modals or new windows. All new flows happen inside the sidebar or as a 4th nav
item. If something needs more space, it opens within the sidebar (replacing the current
sidebar content), not as a popup.
- Sidebar width is fixed at ~537px. Design all sidebar content to work within this width
without horizontal scrolling.
- Do not redesign existing screens. The Home, Mouse, and Joystick screens are
finalized. New sidebar panels that extend them should add to them, not change existing
layout or card positions.
- Setter Mode: If any new floating elements are added to Phase Mode, they must be
draggable in Setter Mode (show a drag handle or cursor cue when Setter Mode is active,
nothing otherwise).
- Primary color #B3A7FF is for active/selected states and primary action buttons only.
Don't use it purely decoratively.

-- 12 of 12 --

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<title>PixelPlay — Feature 3: Profile Switching</title>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
html, body { width: 100%; height: 100%; overflow: hidden; background: #0d0d18; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; }

/* ── TOKENS ── */
:root {
  --accent:       #B3A7FF;
  --accent-deep:  #9381f5;
  --accent-glow:  rgba(179,167,255,0.25);
  --text-1:       rgba(255,255,255,0.92);
  --text-2:       rgba(255,255,255,0.45);
  --text-3:       rgba(255,255,255,0.28);
  --card:         rgba(255,255,255,0.14);
  --card-border:  rgba(255,255,255,0.18);
  --card-strong:  rgba(255,255,255,0.22);
  --danger:       #ff6b6b;
  --spring:       cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1);
  --ease:         cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1);
}

/* ── STAGE & CANVAS ── */
#stage { position: fixed; inset: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 16px; }
#canvas {
  width: 1612px; height: 906px;
  position: relative; overflow: hidden;
  transform-origin: center center;
  border-radius: 12px;
  box-shadow: 0 40px 100px rgba(0,0,0,0.7);
}

/* background */
.bg { position: absolute; inset: 0; }
.bg img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; position: absolute; inset: 0; }
.bg-orb { position: absolute; border-radius: 50%; filter: blur(100px); pointer-events: none; }
.orb-a { width: 600px; height: 600px; background: #5b4dcc; opacity: 0.22; top: -150px; left: -100px; animation: orbFloat 8s ease-in-out infinite alternate; }
.orb-b { width: 450px; height: 450px; background: #2a4dcc; opacity: 0.18; bottom: -80px; right: 100px; animation: orbFloat 10s ease-in-out infinite alternate-reverse; }
@keyframes orbFloat { from { transform: translate(0,0); } to { transform: translate(30px,20px); } }

/* ── TITLE BAR ── */
.title-bar {
  position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 38px;
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.3); backdrop-filter: blur(20px);
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;
  padding: 0 16px; z-index: 40;
}
.tb-left { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; }
.conn-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: #4caf50; }
.tb-title { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); font-weight: 500; }
.tb-profile-badge {
  font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--accent);
  padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 20px;
  border: 1px solid rgba(179,167,255,0.35);
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.08);
  transition: all 300ms ease;
}
.tb-profile-badge.pulse { animation: profilePulse 600ms ease; }
@keyframes profilePulse {
  0%   { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.08); box-shadow: none; }
  40%  { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.35); box-shadow: 0 0 16px rgba(179,167,255,0.5); }
  100% { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.08); box-shadow: none; }
}
.tb-win-btns { display: flex; gap: 6px; }
.tb-win-btn { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; cursor: pointer; }
.tb-win-btn.min { background: #f5a623; }
.tb-win-btn.max { background: #7ed321; }
.tb-win-btn.cls { background: #d0021b; }

/* ── NAV BAR ── */
.nav-bar {
  position: absolute; top: 38px; left: 0; right: 0;
  height: 60px; display: flex; align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between; padding: 0 32px;
  z-index: 30;
}
.nav-logo { display: flex; align-items: center; }
.nav-logo img { height: 20px; }

.nav-pill {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 2px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.18);
  border-radius: 100px; padding: 4px;
  position: relative; height: 46px;
}
.nav-pill-slider {
  position: absolute; top: 4px;
  height: calc(100% - 8px);
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.92);
  border-radius: 100px;
  transition: left 500ms var(--spring), width 500ms var(--spring);
  pointer-events: none; z-index: 0;
}
.nav-item {
  position: relative; z-index: 1;
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
  padding: 0 16px; height: 38px; border-radius: 100px;
  cursor: pointer; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--text-2); transition: color 200ms;
  white-space: nowrap; border: none; background: transparent; font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.nav-item.active { color: #1a1a1a; }
.nav-item svg { flex-shrink: 0; }

/* profile pill in navbar */
.nav-profile-pill {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px;
  height: 36px; padding: 0 14px; border-radius: 100px;
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.12);
  border: 1px solid rgba(179,167,255,0.3);
  cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms, border-color 200ms;
  font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--accent);
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.nav-profile-pill:hover { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.22); border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.5); }
.nav-profile-pill.pulse { animation: profilePulse 600ms ease; }
.profile-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--accent); flex-shrink: 0; }

.nav-right { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; }
.icon-btn {
  width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 10px;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  cursor: pointer; color: var(--text-2); transition: background 150ms, color 150ms;
  border: none;
}
.icon-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.14); color: var(--text-1); }

/* ── MAIN CONTENT ── */
.screen-body {
  position: absolute; top: 98px; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  padding: 40px;
}
.screen-center { text-align: center; }
.screen-title { font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-1); margin-bottom: 8px; }
.screen-sub { font-size: 14px; color: var(--text-2); }

/* Device status cards (home screen) */
.cards-row { display: flex; gap: 16px; margin-top: 48px; justify-content: center; }
.status-card {
  width: 210px;
  background: var(--card); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
  border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 16px;
  padding: 20px; cursor: default;
  animation: cardIn 400ms var(--spring) both;
}
.status-card:nth-child(1) { animation-delay: 0ms; }
.status-card:nth-child(2) { animation-delay: 200ms; }
.status-card:nth-child(3) { animation-delay: 400ms; }
@keyframes cardIn { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(40px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateY(0); } }
.sc-label { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); margin-bottom: 6px; }
.sc-value { font-size: 32px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--accent); }
.sc-sub { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); margin-top: 4px; }

/* Settings trigger */
.settings-trigger {
  display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
  height: 44px; padding: 0 20px; border-radius: 12px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border: 1px solid var(--card-border);
  color: var(--text-1); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer; margin-top: 32px;
  transition: background 150ms; font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.settings-trigger:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.16); }

/* ── OVERLAY ── */
.overlay {
  position: absolute; inset: 0; z-index: 50;
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); backdrop-filter: blur(2px);
  opacity: 0; pointer-events: none;
  transition: opacity 300ms ease;
}
.overlay.open { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; }

/* ── SIDEBAR ── */
.sidebar {
  position: absolute; top: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;
  width: 537px; z-index: 60;
  background: rgba(20,18,40,0.75);
  backdrop-filter: blur(40px) saturate(150%);
  border-left: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
  transform: translateX(100%);
  transition: transform 300ms var(--ease);
  display: flex; flex-direction: column;
}
.sidebar.open { transform: translateX(0); }

.sb-header {
  height: 64px; flex-shrink: 0;
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
  padding: 0 24px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
}
.sb-back-btn {
  width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 9px;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border: none;
  cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0;
  transition: background 150ms, opacity 150ms;
  color: var(--text-1); font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.sb-back-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); }
.sb-back-btn.hidden { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }
.sb-title-text { flex: 1; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-1); }
.sb-close-btn {
  width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 8px;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border: none;
  cursor: pointer; color: var(--text-2); font-size: 14px;
  transition: background 150ms, color 150ms; font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.sb-close-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.14); color: var(--text-1); }

.sb-body { flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; position: relative; }
.sb-view { padding: 20px 24px; }
.sb-view.hidden { display: none; }

/* section label */
.sec-label {
  font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-3);
  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  margin-bottom: 10px; margin-top: 4px;
}

/* ── PROFILE LIST ── */
.profile-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.09);
  border-radius: 12px; padding: 14px 14px 14px 16px;
  margin-bottom: 8px; cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 150ms, border-color 150ms;
}
.profile-row:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); }
.profile-row.active-profile {
  border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.45);
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.08);
}
.profile-dot-indicator {
  width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.2); flex-shrink: 0;
  transition: background 200ms;
}
.profile-row.active-profile .profile-dot-indicator { background: var(--accent); }
.profile-info { flex: 1; }
.profile-name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-1); }
.profile-meta { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); margin-top: 2px; }
.profile-badge {
  font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--accent);
  padding: 2px 9px; border-radius: 20px;
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.15);
  border: 1px solid rgba(179,167,255,0.3);
}
.profile-actions { display: flex; gap: 4px; }
.row-icon-btn {
  width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 8px;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  background: transparent; border: none; cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--text-2); transition: background 150ms, color 150ms;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.row-icon-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); color: var(--text-1); }
.row-icon-btn.danger:hover { background: rgba(255,107,107,0.15); color: var(--danger); }

/* new profile btn */
.new-profile-btn {
  width: 100%; height: 44px; border-radius: 12px;
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.12);
  border: 1px solid rgba(179,167,255,0.25);
  color: var(--accent); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 7px;
  margin-top: 8px; transition: background 150ms;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.new-profile-btn:hover { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.22); }

/* ── PROFILE EDITOR ── */
.name-field {
  width: 100%; height: 44px; border-radius: 10px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
  color: var(--text-1); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600;
  padding: 0 14px; margin-bottom: 16px;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif; outline: none;
}
.name-field:focus { border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.5); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.11); }

.summary-chips { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 20px; }
.chip {
  font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2);
  padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 20px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
}

/* app rule row */
.app-rule-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
  padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 10px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}
.app-icon {
  width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 8px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  font-size: 16px; flex-shrink: 0;
}
.app-info { flex: 1; }
.app-name { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-1); }
.app-process { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-2); margin-top: 1px; }

/* add rule btn */
.add-rule-btn {
  width: 100%; height: 40px; border-radius: 10px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  border: 1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
  color: var(--text-2); font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px;
  margin-top: 6px; transition: background 150ms, color 150ms, border-color 150ms;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.add-rule-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); color: var(--text-1); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.35); }

/* editor footer */
.editor-footer {
  padding: 16px 24px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  display: flex; gap: 10px; flex-shrink: 0;
}
.btn-save {
  flex: 1; height: 44px; border-radius: 12px;
  background: var(--accent); border: none;
  color: white; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700;
  cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms, transform 150ms;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.btn-save:hover { background: var(--accent-deep); transform: translateY(-1px); }
.btn-cancel {
  height: 44px; padding: 0 20px; border-radius: 12px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
  color: var(--text-2); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif;
}
.btn-cancel:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); color: var(--text-1); }

/* ── APP SEARCH ── */
.search-field {
  width: 100%; height: 44px; border-radius: 10px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
  color: var(--text-1); font-size: 14px;
  padding: 0 14px; margin-bottom: 14px;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif; outline: none;
}
.search-field:focus { border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.5); }
.search-field::placeholder { color: var(--text-2); }

.running-app-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
  padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 10px;
  cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms;
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}
.running-app-row:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); }
.running-app-row.selected { background: rgba(179,167,255,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(179,167,255,0.3); }

.manual-entry-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
  padding: 12px 12px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  margin-top: 8px; cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 13px; color: var(--text-2);
  transition: color 150ms;
}
.manual-entry-row:hover { color: var(--text-1); }

.cancel-link {
  font-size: 13px; color: var(--text-2); cursor: pointer;
  padding: 12px 0; text-align: center; display: block;
  transition: color 150ms;
}
.cancel-link:hover { color: var(--text-1); }

/* ── QUICK SWITCH PROFILE ROWS (WF1 sidebar) ── */
.qs-profile-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
  padding: 14px 16px; border-radius: 12px;
  cursor: pointer; margin-bottom: 6px;
  border: 1.5px solid transparent;
  transition: background 150ms, border-color 150ms;
}
.qs-profile-row:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); }
.qs-profile-row.active-profile {
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.1);
  border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.4);
}
.qs-check {
  width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); flex-shrink: 0;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  transition: background 200ms;
}
.qs-profile-row.active-profile .qs-check {
  background: var(--accent);
}
.qs-name { flex: 1; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-1); }
.qs-meta { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); }

/* ── AUTO-SWITCH SIMULATOR (WF3) ── */
.sim-panel {
  position: absolute; top: 110px; left: 40px;
  width: 340px;
  background: var(--card); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
  border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 16px;
  padding: 20px; z-index: 20;
}
.sim-title { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-1); margin-bottom: 4px; }
.sim-sub { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); margin-bottom: 16px; }
.sim-app-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
  padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 10px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.09);
  margin-bottom: 6px; cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 200ms, border-color 200ms;
}
.sim-app-row:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.18); }
.sim-app-row.triggering {
  border-color: rgba(179,167,255,0.5);
  background: rgba(179,167,255,0.1);
  animation: simRowPulse 600ms ease;
}
@keyframes simRowPulse {
  0%   { box-shadow: none; }
  50%  { box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(179,167,255,0.35); }
  100% { box-shadow: none; }
}
.sim-app-name { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-1); }
.sim-app-sub { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-2); margin-top: 1px; }
.sim-note { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-3); margin-top: 12px; text-align: center; }

/* Profile status panel (WF3) */
.profile-status-panel {
  position: absolute; top: 110px; right: 60px;
  width: 320px;
  background: var(--card); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
  border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 16px;
  padding: 20px; z-index: 20;
}
.psp-label { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-2); margin-bottom: 6px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-weight: 600; }
.psp-active-name {
  font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--accent);
  margin-bottom: 10px; transition: opacity 200ms;
}
.psp-rows { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; margin-top: 12px; }
.psp-row {
  display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;
  font-size: 13px; padding: 6px 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
}
.psp-row-label { color: var(--text-2); }
.psp-row-val { color: var(--text-1); font-weight: 600; }

/* Switch log */
.switch-log {
  position: absolute; bottom: 60px; left: 40px;
  width: 340px;
  background: var(--card); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(120%);
  border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 14px;
  padding: 14px 16px; z-index: 20;
}
.log-title { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); margin-bottom: 10px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }
.log-entry {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
  font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-2); padding: 4px 0;
}
.log-entry.recent { color: var(--accent); }
.log-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--text-3); flex-shrink: 0; }
.log-entry.recent .log-dot { background: var(--accent); }

/* ── TOAST ── */
.toast {
  position: absolute; bottom: 32px; left: 50%;
  transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(80px);
  z-index: 100;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.14);
  backdrop-filter: blur(30px) saturate(140%);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.22);
  border-radius: 16px; padding: 14px 22px;
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
  font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-1);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
  opacity: 0; pointer-events: none;
  transition: transform 350ms var(--spring), opacity 300ms ease;
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.toast.show { transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0); opacity: 1; }
.toast-icon {
  width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--accent); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

/* ── WORKFLOW SWITCHER (outside canvas) ── */
.wf-switcher {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.07);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  border-radius: 100px; padding: 3px;
  position: relative;
}
.wf-slider {
  position: absolute; top: 3px;
  height: calc(100% - 6px);
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
  border-radius: 100px;
  transition: left 400ms var(--spring), width 400ms var(--spring);
  pointer-events: none;
}
.wf-btn {
  position: relative; z-index: 1;
  padding: 7px 18px; border-radius: 100px;
  border: none; background: transparent;
  font-family: 'Inter',sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); cursor: pointer;
  transition: color 200ms; white-space: nowrap;
}
.wf-btn.active { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); }

/* stagger reveal for sidebar items */
.stagger { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); animation: staggerIn 300ms ease both; }
@keyframes staggerIn { to { opacity:1; transform:translateY(0); } }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<div id="stage">
  <div id="canvas">
    <!-- BG -->
    <div class="bg">
      <img src="assets/images/background.png" alt="">
      <div class="bg-orb orb-a"></div>
      <div class="bg-orb orb-b"></div>
    </div>

    <!-- Title bar -->
    <div class="title-bar">
      <div class="tb-left">
        <div class="conn-dot"></div>
        <span class="tb-title">PixelPlay</span>
        <!-- WF3: title bar profile badge -->
        <div class="tb-profile-badge" id="tb-badge" style="display:none">Design</div>
      </div>
      <div class="tb-win-btns">
        <div class="tb-win-btn min"></div>
        <div class="tb-win-btn max"></div>
        <div class="tb-win-btn cls"></div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Nav bar -->
    <div class="nav-bar">
      <div class="nav-logo"><img src="assets/icons/Logo.svg" alt="Pixelplay"></div>

      <div class="nav-pill" id="nav-pill">
        <div class="nav-pill-slider" id="nav-slider"></div>
        <button class="nav-item active" data-nav="home" onclick="navClick('home',this)">
          <svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 12 13.5" fill="currentColor"><path d="M1.5 12L3.75 12L3.75 7.5L8.25 7.5L8.25 12L10.5 12L10.5 5.25L6 1.875L1.5 5.25Z M0 13.5L0 4.5L6 0L12 4.5L12 13.5L6.75 13.5L6.75 9L5.25 9L5.25 13.5Z"/></svg>
          Home
        </button>
        <button class="nav-item" data-nav="mouse" onclick="navClick('mouse',this)">
